purpose: Categorizes correlation regime transitions by structural driver to predict duration: fiscal stalemate transitions (fiscal expansion conflicting with monetary tightening) last 5-7 years, approximately 2-3x longer than clean monetary transitions (12-36 months).
components: Clean monetary transition: CB-driven, typically 12-36 months, resolved by credible policy shift
Fiscal stalemate transition: fiscal expansion vs. monetary tightening, 5-7 years, requires crystallizing event
Three documented analogues: US 1966-71 (5yr), UK 1972-77 (5yr), Italy 2008-14 (6yr)
Current episode classified as fiscal stalemate: started Q1 2022, 4 years elapsed, suggesting 1-3 more years
Crystallizing event (recession, financial stress, fiscal reform) typically required for resolution
current_assessment: Judged more appropriate than generalist_01's 12-36 month window for the current configuration per pair_0. Current 4-year transition is within range for fiscal stalemate. However, the current fiscal position (6-7% deficit, ~125% debt/GDP) exceeds all three precedents, making duration extrapolation uncertain.
limitations: Only n=3 analogues with varying institutional contexts (US, UK, Italy)
Current fiscal position exceeds all three precedents — may extend or compress the duration
Italy analogue complicated by eurozone membership (no sovereign currency)
Does not account for structural novelties (50% passive AUM, AI, $800B basis trade)
Crystallizing event requirement is difficult to predict ex ante
